summary: This epic, 'Group Creation and Invitations,' establishes the foundational user flow for creating private, shared groups and inviting members via a link or code. It is the critical first step for users to begin building their collective memory map. The flow must be simple, secure, and feel native to iOS, setting a high-quality baseline for subsequent features.
user_pains: Existing photo albums and chat logs lose the context of 'what happened here with us?'
Searching through years of messages to recall a shared memory at a specific location is inefficient and frustrating.
Public map review apps are for discovery, not for preserving private group memories over time.
Tools for couples are too narrow for friend groups, and social feeds are too public and ephemeral for intimate memories.
hig_risks: The group creation flow, with its text inputs and image picker, could fail HIG if controls are smaller than 44x44pt or if text contrast is poor.
Invitation screens must present links and codes clearly, respecting Dynamic Type and not being obscured by the keyboard.
The initial group setup screen must respect all device safe areas (notches, Dynamic Island) to avoid looking like a non-native web view.
Failing to use standard iOS patterns for this simple form-based flow could increase App Store review risk and create a jarring user experience.
design_references: The emotional clarity and intimacy of a private diary app.
The spatial confidence and calm hierarchy of a strong native map experience like Apple Maps.
The polish and interaction quality expected from top-tier iOS productivity or memory products.
The simple, focused onboarding flows of modern social apps that prioritize getting the user to the core value quickly.
